messages.content: Consumerism, which had its peak in the mid-20th century in the United States, set the stage for households. - rephrase this
Households were set on stage by consumerism, reaching its peak in the United States during the mid-20th century.
At the time, consumerism emphasized the benefits of capitalism in increasing society's living standards. - rephrase this
During that period, consumerism highlighted how capitalism could enhance the living standards of society.
Nowadays, the value system has changed significantly from the peak of consumerism, making visible changes in the combination of work and personal life.- rephrase this
In modern times, the value system has undergone significant changes since the height of consumerism, resulting in noticeable shifts in the balance between work and personal life.
2. What are the key factors in the growth of digital nomadism among Poles? - rephrase this
What are the main contributors to the rise of digital nomadism among Polish individuals?
The research was carried out using two methods: literature analysis and in-depth interviews.- rephrase this
Two approaches were employed to conduct the research: literature analysis and in-depth interviews.
The concept of work on the go related to social mobility has been present in the literature since the 1980s. - rephrase this
The concept of mobile work associated with social mobility has been a recurring theme in literature since the 1980s.
The rise of remote work has contributed to the growing importance of this concept.- rephrase this
The increasing significance of this concept can be attributed to the growth of remote work.
It is thanks to them that work becomes easier. - rephrase this
They are responsible for making work more effortless.

The Internet is an indispensable tool for digital nomads, who treat work as a fraction of their lives and spend most of their time exploring new places, sharing experiences and cooperating. - rephrase this

For digital nomads, the Internet is an essential tool, allowing them to view work as just a portion of their lives. They dedicate the majority of their time to exploring new locations, sharing experiences, and collaborating.
According to Wang, digital nomads are remote workers who choose to work independently of their location and live a life of constant travel. - rephrase this
As per Wang's research, digital nomads are individuals engaged in remote work who deliberately opt for a location-independent lifestyle, characterized by perpetual travel.

The definition of digital nomadism is also found in the State of Independence of America report, which states that there are digital nomads who have been traveling for many years, regularly visiting multiple countries located on different continents (State of Independence of America, 2018). - rephrase this

The definition of digital nomadism can also be sourced from the State of Independence of America report, which highlights the existence of long-term digital nomads who engage in regular travel across multiple countries spanning various continents (State of Independence of America, 2018).
Another type of digital nomads, are individuals who live this lifestyle only during shorter periods, which may be sabbaticals or working on vacation. - rephrase this
Another category of digital nomads comprises individuals who adopt this lifestyle for shorter durations, such as sabbaticals or working while on vacation.
Another type of digital nomads, are individuals who also travel, but their trips are much shorter. Trips of this type can be sabbaticals or working vacations. - rephrase this
There is another subgroup of digital nomads who engage in shorter travel periods. These individuals also embark on journeys, which could be sabbaticals or working vacations.

There are many definitions, but the authors distinguish a few basic characteristics of digital nomads. They are: 
- being constantly on the move and highly mobile,
- doing remote work in digital form on a computer,
- mostly young age,
- using tools that enable remote work, 
- a lifestyle of exploring new places and adventure.
rephrase above

Despite the existence of numerous definitions, authors have identified several key characteristics that distinguish digital nomads, including:
- Constantly being on the move and embodying high mobility.
- Engaging in remote work conducted via digital platforms and computers.
- Predominantly belonging to the younger age demographic.
- Utilizing tools and technologies that facilitate remote work.
- Embracing a lifestyle centered around discovering new places and seeking adventure.
Most often their destination is semi-peripheral places that provide a minimum of working conditions, but most importantly have a climate that allows them to develop their passions. - rephrase this
Typically, digital nomads tend to gravitate towards semi-peripheral locations that offer basic working conditions, while also prioritizing climates conducive to pursuing their interests and passions.
Neo-nomads are defined as a new generation of information-technology professionals, entrepreneurs and freelancers (Naz, 2017). - rephrase this
According to Naz (2017), neo-nomads are characterized as a fresh wave of information technology professionals, entrepreneurs, and freelancers.

According to Jacobs and Gusseklo, it is not possible to top-down define and categorize the mobility of digital nomads, as only individuals can unequivocally declare membership in this group (Jacobs, Gussekloo, 2016). - rephrase this

Jacobs and Gusseklo argued that the mobility of digital nomads cannot be effectively defined and categorized from a top-down approach, as only individuals themselves have the authority to unequivocally claim membership in this group (Jacobs, Gussekloo, 2016).
These are tourists who usually travel only with a backpack and are technologically advanced. - rephrase this
These individuals are technologically advanced tourists who typically travel with only a backpack.
They use technology as a connectivity tool to plan and organize their trip and communicate with social networks (Jarvis, Peel, 2010). - rephrase this
Jarvis and Peel (2010) describe how these individuals utilize technology as a means of connectivity, using it to plan and arrange their trips as well as stay connected with social networks.
In recent years, the increasing age of people choosing to start a family has been evident around the world. - rephrase this
Across the globe, there has been a noticeable trend in recent years towards individuals opting to start a family at an older age.
Richards outlined the criteria for flashpackers: they travel an average of 62 days a year and take about four trips in five years (Richards, 2015). - rephrase this
According to Richards (2015), flashpackers can be characterized by their average annual travel duration of 62 days and an estimated total of four trips within a span of five years.

A more general group of people who may be associated with digital nomadism are those who work remotely. Remote work is also referred to as working from home, virtual work, distributed work. - rephrase this

A broader category of individuals who could be linked to digital nomadism includes those who engage in remote work. Remote work, also known as working from home, virtual work, or distributed work, encompasses this group.

The most common assumption is that it is a working arrangement in a traditional environment, in which employees spend part of their time away from the physical workplace, such as at home, and communicate with the help of computer technology. - rephrase this

The prevailing assumption is that remote work constitutes a work arrangement within a conventional setting, where employees allocate a portion of their time away from the physical workplace, such as their homes, while relying on computer technology for communication.
Their personality can be described as entrepreneurial, focused on profit maximization, while a more important value for a digital nomad will be adventure (Schad, 2022). - rephrase this

According to Schad (2022), the personality traits of digital nomads can be characterized as entrepreneurial and oriented towards profit maximization. However, an even more significant value for digital nomads is the pursuit of adventure.
Due to the complexity of the concept of digital nomadism and the short time it has existed, no studies have yet been created to examine the scale of the phenomenon. - rephrase this

Given the intricate nature of the digital nomadism concept and its relatively recent emergence, there have been no studies conducted thus far that specifically explore the magnitude of this phenomenon.
Nevertheless, it is a phenomenon that is gaining in importance and is increasingly important to society. - rephrase this
Nonetheless, digital nomadism is an increasingly significant phenomenon that holds growing importance within society.

As a second research method, in addition to the literature review, an individual, in-depth interview was used with people who identify themselves as digital nomads and whose lifestyles are consistent with the main characteristics listed by the authors of the definition. - rephrase this

As a supplementary research method alongside the literature review, individual in-depth interviews were conducted with individuals who self-identify as digital nomads and whose lifestyles align with the key characteristics outlined by the authors of the definition.
Determination of digital nomad belonging. - rephrase this
Identification of digital nomad affiliation.
Respondents were also asked whether they felt they belonged to this group understood as a global subculture, but whether they were individual units. - rephrase this
Respondents were also queried about their sense of belonging to this global subculture, seeking to discern whether they viewed themselves as individual entities or part of a larger collective.

On social media, digital nomads show positive aspects of their nomadic lifestyle, but respondents' answers show that factors significantly influencing their choice of such a lifestyle are negative. - rephrase this

Digital nomads tend to showcase the positive aspects of their nomadic lifestyles on social media. However, insights gathered from respondents indicate that negative factors play a significant role in influencing their decision to adopt such a lifestyle.
In sociology, the concept of home is related to the notion of the first place, that is, the place where an individual lives with the rest of the household. - rephrase this
In sociology, the concept of home is closely connected to the idea of the primary location, which refers to the place where an individual resides with their household members.
As human beings, however, they have needs related to at least symbolic belonging to a place, or human interaction. - rephrase this
Nevertheless, as human beings, individuals possess needs that are intertwined with the desire for a sense of belonging to a place, as well as the need for human interaction.
More important, however, is the aspect of the availability of such sites and their public accessibility. - rephrase this
However, of greater significance is the aspect of the availability of such locations and their accessibility to the general public.

Considering the third places, there is a clear division between those related to classically understood places of integration, such as a pub or disco, and those related to the natural environment. - rephrase this

When considering third places, a distinct division can be observed between those traditionally associated with social integration, such as pubs or nightclubs, and those connected to natural environments.
Among the responses were mainly indications that the climate of the location would be significantly different from the temperate climate prevailing in Poland. - rephrase this
Among the responses, the prevalent indication was that the climate of the chosen location would vary significantly from the temperate climate commonly found in Poland.
"Second places" or work space is found in transportation and any place with internet access that offers a place to sit. - rephrase this
"Second places," or workspaces, can be found in transportation as well as any location with internet access that provides seating arrangements.
In the literature, the characteristics of digital nomads include: young age, use of Internet technologies for remote work, and following the desire to experience adventure. - rephrase this

The literature highlights several characteristics commonly associated with digital nomads, including their young age, utilization of internet technologies for remote work, and a strong inclination towards seeking adventurous experiences.

Digital nomads choose this lifestyle because they are not satisfied with the living conditions in Poland, which include many aspects: the cold climate, the cost of living, natural conditions, or the desire to visit different places. - rephrase this

Digital nomads opt for this lifestyle due to various factors associated with their dissatisfaction with living conditions in Poland. These factors encompass the cold climate, high cost of living, natural surroundings, and a desire to explore diverse locations.
